plan_metadata._status_values: draft | pending-approval | queued | in-progress | done | failed
plan_metadata._comment: task_id and task_name must match the originating request file: N-short-descriptive-name.md

dependencies._comment: required_tasks lists task IDs that must be 'done' before this plan can execute. packages lists any new dependencies to install. affected_modules lists project modules this plan touches.

stages.name: Stage Name
stages.description: What this stage accomplishes and why it exists as a discrete unit of work.
stages.rollback_plan: Specific steps to revert this stage if it fails.
stages._complexity_values: small | medium | large — if 'large', consider splitting into smaller stages

_comment_option_b: How spec/doc updates are structured depends on plan size. See agent-workflow.md for full rules.
Spec and doc updates are INLINE STEPS at the end of the single stage.
The stage's blast radius must include the spec/doc files that may need updating.
Do NOT add separate stages below — the single stage handles everything.
Include spec/doc files in the stage's context_files and output_files.
Add spec and doc updates as SEPARATE FINAL STAGES using the entries below.
Renumber stage_id and instruction_file to fit your plan's stage count.
Each gets its own commit, blast radius, and verification.
If no changes are needed for a given stage, mark it 'skipped' with a justification.

stages._comment: MULTI-STAGE ONLY — Delete this entry for single-stage plans.
stages.name: Spec Updates
stages.description: Update agent-development/agent-specs/ files to reflect any architectural, structural, or convention changes introduced by this plan.
stages.rollback_plan: Revert spec files to their pre-plan state via git checkout.

stages._comment: MULTI-STAGE ONLY — Delete this entry for single-stage plans.
stages.name: Documentation Updates
stages.description: Update human-facing documentation (README.md, DEVELOPMENT-GUIDE.md, and any other relevant docs) to reflect changes introduced by this plan.
stages.rollback_plan: Revert documentation files to their pre-plan state via git checkout.
